annotations.label: Lindemann–Weierstrass theorem
annotations.note: Only the analytic preparation lemma is in Mathlib; the main Lindemann–Weierstrass linear-independence statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalent formulation (Baker)
annotations.note: Equivalent to the main theorem; only the analytic preparation lemma exists, not the algebraic-independence/linear-independence conclusion.

annotations.label: Transcendence of e

anchor.snippet: In particular, e 1 = e is transcendental.

annotations.note: No declaration `Transcendental ℤ (Real.exp 1)` (or analogue) found in Mathlib via grep, loogle, or semantic search.

annotations.label: Transcendence of e^α via second formulation

anchor.snippet: is linearly independent over the algebraic numbers and in particular e α cannot be algebraic

annotations.note: Transcendence of e^α for algebraic α≠0 is not formalized; depends on the un-formalized main Lindemann–Weierstrass theorem.

annotations.label: Transcendence of π

anchor.snippet: To prove that π is transcendental, we prove that it is not algebraic.

annotations.note: Only irrationality of π is formalized (`irrational_pi`); transcendence of π is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transcendence of sin, cos, tan of algebraic numbers

anchor.snippet: if α is a non-zero algebraic number then sin(α), cos(α), tan(α) and their hyperbolic counterparts are also transcendental

annotations.note: No declaration asserting transcendence of sin/cos/tan/sinh/cosh/tanh at nonzero algebraic arguments found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic Lindemann–Weierstrass Conjecture
annotations.note: p-adic version is an open conjecture; not stated or formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modular conjecture

anchor.value: Modular conjecture — Let q 1 , ..., q n

annotations.note: The modular-j analogue conjecture is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lindemann–Weierstrass Theorem (Baker's reformulation)
annotations.note: Baker's reformulation is not formalized; only the analytic approximation lemma is present.

annotations.label: Lemma A
annotations.note: Lemma A's analytic content is captured by `exp_polynomial_approx`, but the full propositional statement (no rational vanishing sum) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lemma B
annotations.note: Lemma B (with algebraic coefficients) is not formalized; only related analytic infrastructure exists.

annotations.label: Lemma A implies e is irrational

anchor.snippet: Lemma A is sufficient to prove that e is irrational

annotations.note: Irrationality of e via Lemma A is not present in Mathlib; no `irrational_exp_one` or equivalent was found.

annotations.label: Lemma A implies π is irrational

anchor.snippet: Lemma A also suffices to prove that π is irrational

annotations.note: Mathlib has `irrational_pi`, but it is proved by Niven's method rather than as a consequence of Lemma A.

annotations.label: Lemma B implies e is transcendental

anchor.snippet: Lemma B is sufficient to prove that e is transcendental

annotations.note: Transcendence of e (via Lemma B or otherwise) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lemma B implies π is transcendental

anchor.snippet: Lemma B also suffices to prove that π is transcendental

annotations.note: Transcendence of π is not formalized in Mathlib (only irrationality is).

annotations.label: Transcendence degree reformulation

anchor.snippet: In other words, the extension field

annotations.note: Restates the theorem as: the field extension generated by e^{α_i} has transcendence degree n over ℚ.

annotations.label: Hermite's theorem (integer-exponent special case)

anchor.snippet: Charles Hermite first proved the simpler theorem where the α i exponents are required to be rational integers and linear independence is only assured over the rational integers

annotations.note: Hermite's special case (integer exponents, ℤ-linear independence) is not isolated as a Mathlib declaration.
